data.title: After the number of nodes dropped by 70%, Solana is now in a panic.
data.description:
Author: momo, chaincatcher

The number of Solana nodes has decreased by 70% from its historical high. In early April, according to Solana Compass data, its validator count plummeted from 2,560 in March 2023 to approximately 756; during the same period, the Satoshi Index dropped from 31 to 20, a decrease of 35%, indicating a corresponding weakening of decentralization.

The number of Solana nodes has decreased by 70% from its historical high. In early April, according to Solana Compass data, its validator count plummeted from 2,560 in March 2023 to approximately 756; during the same period, the Satoshi Index dropped from 31 to 20, a decrease of 35%, indicating a corresponding weakening of decentralization.

data.title: My final letter to DeepSeek urging them to update!
data.description:
Hu YanpingShanghai University of Finance and EconomicsDistinguished Professor of Shanghai University of Finance and Economics, Author of the Smart Economy Series Report

Recently, the article "People Miss DeepSeek" went viral, mentioning that DeepSeek has driven down the cost of large-scale global models, allowing users and industries to enjoy cheaper tokens.

The key issue is that the rampant "token burning" of intelligent agent applications like "crayfish" has further increased user costs. In this situation, the responsibility of driving cost reduction and efficiency improvement across the entire industry has once again fallen on DeepSeek's shoulders. It's been over a year since the release of DeepSeek V3 and R1, and the outside world originally expected DeepSeek V4 to make a splash during this year's Spring Festival, but those hopes were ultimately dashed. However, judging from recent events such as system outages and the launch of expert mode, it seems that DeepSeek V4 may be getting closer to us. Therefore, this may be the last time we "urge" DeepSeek to release an update. In this letter urging me to update, I want to talk to those friends who miss DeepSeek about topics such as the narrative of AI in China, the wave of technological evolution, ecosystem competition, and token economics. The narrative of AI in China has changed. In the Spring Festival of 2025, DeepSeek R1 made its debut with low cost, high performance, and open source, reaching its peak upon release. It not only leads the field of large-scale domestic models but also sweeps the world. Internet platforms, IT giants, and various industries have all adopted it and embraced open source, with all sorts of DeepSeek all-in-one machines trying to steal the spotlight. During that period, when discussing AI in China, DeepSeek was invariably mentioned. It's no exaggeration to say that even elderly people on the street were likely talking about, or even using, this domestically developed AI assistant. However, over the past year, the AI ​​industry and China's AI narrative have become vastly different: a three-pronged narrative surrounding AI—the "Chinese team," the "Chinese chain," and the "Chinese ring"—has taken shape. DeepSeek's solo AI narrative for China has lost its luster. Therefore, from this perspective, when looking at large-scale models and AI, what everyone lacks is not just computing power and electricity, but also a window of opportunity. Regarding the "Chinese team," I summarize it as "(3+1)+6+N." The "3+1" refers to the four major companies: ByteDance, Alibaba, Tencent, and Baidu. The latter three are the three giants of the internet era, the well-known BAT (Baidu, Alibaba, Tencent). The number "6" corresponds to the "Six Little Tigers" of the big model era—Kimi, Zhipu, MiniMax, Jieyue Xingchen, Baichuan, and Mianbi Intelligence. These companies either completed their IPOs or were on their way to IPOs while DeepSeek was focused on its own research and development. The "Six Little Tigers" originally included Kai-Fu Lee's 01Wanwu, but it began to fall behind in the first "Hundred Models Battle," so we've included Mianbi Intelligence here. However, Mianbi Intelligence's presence has also gradually diminished over the past year. "N" doesn't refer to a single company; it represents other vertical model and professional market AI companies. A total of 10 companies/categories constitute the leading force in China's large model industry. They are no longer isolated players, but a competitive industrial group, and a formidable opponent that DeepSeek must overcome on its path to renewed success. Growing alongside the "Chinese team" is also the "Chinese blockchain"—from chip computing power, clusters/cloud, data corpora, algorithms/models, intelligent agents, and AI application development ecosystem, it has completed the entire chain connection, becoming one of only two countries globally with a complete intelligent technology industry chain. It has the potential to provide more options for global intelligent infrastructure and to offer new public goods for global intelligent inclusion through the export of capabilities in the capabilities economy. This shouldn't be doubted; while DeepSeek R1 certainly established the brand of Chinese models overseas, manufacturers like MiniMax are now also doing very well in overseas markets. Regarding the "China Ring," it covers three aspects: industry, application, and investment—an industrial closed loop from AI to AI4S and then to modern industrial clusters; a market application closed loop from AI technology to thousands of industries and millions of households; and a capital closed loop from early-stage investment to IPO exit. The initial formation of this closed loop not only signifies the successful implementation of artificial intelligence in China but also indicates the interconnection of large and small cycles at different levels of the intelligent economy. From groups and chains to rings, China's AI narrative has already changed. Excluding free marketing strategies by companies, since the beginning of 2026, the Six Little Tigers model has consistently led in token consumption on international platforms such as OpenRouter, with an overall share exceeding half, primarily driven by overseas users. In summary, by 2025, China's open-source capabilities will have reshaped the global landscape of artificial intelligence development. By 2026, China's AI development will have entered a phase of exporting its capabilities. From a global perspective and within the AI ​​industry, the diversification of technological pathways enhances talent mobility and improves supply chain resilience. For downstream application developers, the availability of multiple suppliers translates to stronger bargaining power and lower lock-in risk. Another positive aspect of China's AI narrative is that the market is not yet monopolized by a few oligopolies. This is beneficial for competitive innovation and talent ecosystem development, and also helps to create a cluster advantage in the Sino-US AI competition. The Four Waves Have Passed Chinese classical mythology often says, "One day in heaven is one year on earth." In the year that DeepSeek has been "inactive," four waves of artificial intelligence have passed—programming, multimodal, intelligent agents, and OpenClaw (crayfish). While AI programming tools like GitHub Copilot, Cursor, and Claude Code have swept the developer community, DeepSeek's existence is easily forgotten in the story of Vibe Coding, even though it is also used in programming scenarios. Programming, the underlying driver of artificial intelligence sweeping across all industries and the most essential scenario for developers, is now firmly dominated by companies like Anthropic abroad, while in China it has become a battlefield for companies like Kimi to expand their territory. In this wave of multimodal computing, Gemini 3 Pro and others have shown impressive performance in visual understanding and image generation. Nano Banana is a name that comes to mind, while in video generation, it's ByteDance's Seedance 2.0. DeepSeek, on the other hand, seems to be a slow starter, only beginning its gray-scale testing of a million token contexts with V3.2, and its multimodal capabilities are still far from being realized. Some say that in the field of large-scale models, if a product's technological path is wrong, it will miss an entire era. Is DeepSeek stuck here? It's hard to say. The third wave is Agent-Multi-Agent-Swarm Intelligence. Compared to the understanding and conversational abilities of AI assistants, agents have evolved to the execution level, shifting from "answering questions" to "solving questions"—in the past it was "passive response," now it's "active execution." In this wave, the emergence of products like Manus signifies that AI Agents are moving from concept to reality, while Kimi Agent Swarm has pushed this wave to its climax. In this wave, DeepSeek was primarily used as a model rather than a builder of the agent ecosystem, and the model itself had limited support for agents, tools, and code. By 2026, a wave of action intelligence, represented by OpenClaw and various Claws, Claude Code, and Claude Cowork, began to emerge. Their capabilities had actually transcended the agent level, becoming a takeover-type application operating system—an application AI OS. However, products like OpenClaw were jokingly referred to as "token black holes," as their token consumption per task was tens or even hundreds of times that of traditional conversational AI. This high-input, low-output model faces sustainability challenges in large-scale industrial applications. The product's roughness, instability, and multiple disruptive iterations are akin to a poorly constructed house. Therefore, it's not surprising that some are saying "people miss DeepSeek," given that it has disappeared after several waves of development, and that people need it to drive cost reduction and efficiency improvement in China's large-scale models. However, it must be said that OpenClaw confirms the validity of the logic behind applying AI OS and general-purpose intelligent agents, and that the time is right. It tells everyone that AI is no longer just a tool, but can be an all-powerful, takeover-type agent. So, during the March "shrimp farming craze," you can see how quickly everyone copied each other's methods. To promote local products, everyone started giving away "cyber eggs" (internet-enabled eggs). This is because OpenClaw made major companies, including Anthropic, instantly realize that an all-encompassing application OS and mobile intelligence were within reach. They had brains, could execute tasks, and growing arms and legs to become general-purpose intelligent agents was easy! This is also why Anthropic reacted and countered the fastest, and had the biggest impact on Claw. Claude Code attacked OpenClaw's escape route, and other major companies quickly copied Claude Code and OpenClaw's methods. This is what is happening right now. The reason this is so crucial is that its entry point, immense value, and future ecological dominance are no less than that of models or the previous three waves of innovation. If large models are accumulating power, multimodal approaches are expanding scenarios, and agents represent sowing seeds, then large-scale harvesting of the ecosystem relies on the application of AI OS and general-purpose intelligent agents, which already carries some hints of an endgame and ultimate form. However, the situation may change when we reach the EI (intrinsic intelligence) and II (autonomous intelligence) stages. However, considering OpenClaw's return on investment today, it's unlikely to be the one that will occupy the ecological niche of AI OS and general-purpose intelligent agents. So, in this final letter urging DeepSeek to release more content, we also want to raise a question: Is DeepSeek, by not immediately jumping into these four areas, choosing to conserve its strength, hoping to "make a big splash" with V4 and subsequent foundational models? However, the market never waits. User attention, developer enthusiasm, and capital flows are all being diverted in wave after wave. The competitive barriers in the fields where these four waves occur have risen sharply, and the costs of ecosystem development have also increased significantly. Is DeepSeek's story destined to end with the Spring Festival of 2025? The Arrival of Full Ecosystem Competition

The Arrival of Full Ecosystem Competition

Previously, my view was that leading companies had entered the stage of full ecosystem competition. In this stage, full-stack AI capabilities are the foundation for the next round of competition among giants; the best example is Google.

The Arrival of Full Ecosystem Competition

My previous view was that leading companies had already entered the stage of full ecosystem competition. In this stage, full-stack AI capabilities are the foundation for the next round of competition among giants; Google is the best example.

Previously, my view was that leading companies had entered the stage of full ecosystem competition. In this stage, full-stack AI capabilities are the foundation for the next round of competition among giants; Google is the best example.

The Arrival of Full Ecosystem Competition

Previously, my view was that leading companies had entered the stage of full ecosystem competition.

Expectations for V4 are not the standards of the past

The suspense across the ocean is: to what extent can OpenAI and Anthropic's next-generation models compete? Can Super Apps become application operating systems and general-purpose intelligent agents like the increasingly ecosystem-building Claude Code? And which will be the fastest in coding, the underlying sharp knife of the ecosystem? These three things will influence the major trends this year.

Judging from the current situation, Anthropic's success is almost reaching OpenAI's stronghold. This can be seen from the financial data of the two companies disclosed by the Wall Street Journal. Anthropic may be able to turn a profit before OpenAI.

From the current situation, Anthropic's success is almost reaching OpenAI's stronghold.

From the current situation, Anthropic's success is almost reaching OpenAI's stronghold.

Judging from ... may be able to turn a profit before OpenAI.

data.title: Covenant AI's exit caused TAO's stock to plummet 17%, signaling a crisis for Bittensor.
data.description:
Tao Zhu, Jinse Finance

Summary

On April 9, 2026, Covenant AI, the main subnet developer on Bittensor, announced its withdrawal from the Bittensor network ecosystem. Covenant claims that Bittensor co-founder Jacob Steeves has control over the network and accuses Bittensor's promise of decentralization of being "a lie."

Why is Covenant AI leaving Bittensor? How did Bittensor respond? Why did Covenant AI's withdrawal cause Bittensor to plummet by 17%?

I. Covenant AI's Statement

Covenant AI founder Sam Dare issued a statement on X, pointing out that the reason for Covenant AI's departure from Bittensor is the centralized power of Bittensor co-founder Jacob Steeves. The three-way alliance structure (multi-signature for network upgrades by three people, claiming to the community that this is distributed governance) is just a show of decentralization.

Why is Covenant AI leaving Bittensor?

Why is Covenant AI leaving Bittensor?

I. Covenant AI's Statement

Covenant AI founder Sam Dare issued a statement on X, pointing out that the reason for Covenant AI's departure from Bittensor is the centralized power of Bittensor co-founder Jacob Steeves. The three-way alliance structure (multi-signature for network upgrades by three people, claiming to the community that this is distributed governance) is just a show of decentralization.

On April 9, 2026, Covenant AI announced its withdrawal from Bittensor due to the centralized power of Bittensor co-founder Jacob Steeves.

... Jacob Steeves effectively controls the three-person governing team, capable of unilaterally deploying changes at any time without any procedures or consensus, with the other members acting as legal shields. The specific statement is as follows: Covenant AI is built on a simple belief: the training of cutting-edge AI models should not be controlled by any single entity. Over the past two years, our team has delivered on this vision. Covenant-72B—a model with 72 billion parameters, collaboratively trained by over 70 independent contributors using general-purpose hardware in a permissionless environment—is arguably the largest decentralized large-scale language model (LLM) pre-training project in history. It not only garnered recognition from Nvidia's CEO and was cited by Anthropic's co-founder, but it also propelled the ecosystem we built to achieve a value surge of up to 90%. We never sought attention intentionally. We simply wanted to prove that decentralized training models work. The attention that followed was merely a testament to the power of our work. We hope to provide an unambiguous and clear explanation of what all this means. When a single entity can arbitrarily halt the token distribution of a subnet; can override the owner and forcibly take over its management of its own community space; can blatantly abandon a specific project without any established procedures; and can even distort token sales into a coercive mechanism to force others to comply—this is not true "decentralization." It is merely "centralized control" disguised as "decentralization." Every participant, miner, validator, and investor in this ecosystem must be keenly aware that: This totalitarian power truly exists; it has been exercised by Jacob Steeves (i.e., Const); and its purpose is not to maintain the healthy development of the network, but to re-establish control over a team—a team that has become increasingly independent and difficult to manage. For those whose power is entirely based on the fact that "everyone depends on him," any subnet owner capable of independently building communities, making independent decisions, and operating freely without permission is undoubtedly a potential threat. The problems of decentralization are far more complex than any single event can suggest. Bittensor employs a "three-way alliance" operational architecture: three specific individuals jointly manage multisignature (multisig) permissions related to network upgrades; however, when promoting this architecture to the community, it is embellished as a kind of "distributed governance" model. This is far from the truth. It's nothing more than a clumsy "decentralization" political show. Jacob Steeves actually maintains firm control over the real power of this "three-way alliance," vehemently resisting any substantial decentralization and unilaterally pushing for changes anytime, anywhere—without any established procedures or consensus. As for the nominal members within it, their only value is to act as legal "shields"—in the event of accountability or litigation, they will be the first to bear legal responsibility, while Jacob himself will remain unscathed. This online community talks a lot about so-called "governance" and "decentralization," but has never put them into practice. The reins of power have never left the hands of any one person. This is the crux of the problem. Bittensor's entire core premise—the promise that attracted developers, miners, validators, and investors to this ecosystem—is that no single entity can control it. However, this promise is actually a lie. We cannot continue building on a network where our fundamental promise to investors—that the infrastructure is decentralized and permissionless—is at odds with the reality of its actual governance. We cannot raise funds, recruit talent, or ask the community to contribute resources on a foundation that can be arbitrarily destroyed by anyone. We absolutely do not want to pass this risk on to those who trust us. Therefore, with deep regret and frustration, we hereby announce that Covenant AI is officially withdrawing from the Bittensor network. Over the past few weeks, Jacob Steeves (also known as "Const") has taken a series of actions against the operations of Covenant AI that fundamentally contradict the principles the network claims to uphold. These actions include: suspending the distribution of token rewards (Emissions) to our subnets; stripping us of administrative privileges within our own community channels; unilaterally abandoning our subnet infrastructure; and exerting direct economic pressure at a critical moment in our operational conflict through a large-scale, high-profile token sell-off. These are by no means governance decisions made through transparent, decentralized consensus mechanisms. They are merely punitive retaliations by one person—who has never truly relinquished control of the network, despite having publicly declared to the world that he was no longer responsible for its operation. Covenant AI's mission has never changed. Decentralized, permissionless AI training is not a feature unique to Bittensor; it's a technological capability our team is passionately working to advance. Our research, our team members, our model assets, and our grand vision will all go with us. We are currently working on a series of exciting projects and initiatives, and related news and announcements will be officially released to the public soon. To our community members, mining partners, and every supporter who has contributed computing power, time, and faith to the Covenant-72B project: you have proven that things once deemed "impossible" are indeed entirely achievable. The proof of this great achievement does not reside on a single Discord server, nor is it dependent on the governance architecture of any particular network; it is deeply rooted in our research, our AI models, and the very soul of our team. Wherever we choose our next base of operations, we will continue to work diligently to earn your trust and support. Decentralization is not merely a marketing slogan—it will not be allowed to be arbitrarily overturned or trampled upon when this concept becomes "out of place" or "obstructive" for Const. It is a solemn commitment to every builder, every miner, and every investor—they entered this ecosystem based on the promise that "no individual could possibly do what we are doing now." Either make this system truly decentralized, or stop pretending it is. Sam Dare, Founder of Covenant AI (blockquote) II. Response from Bittensor Co-founder Jacob Steeves (blockquote) Jacob Steeves did not initially respond directly, only saying: This will mark the birth of the first subnets on Bittensor, which will operate in a "headless" mode and exist as a true commodity. Following this, Steeves continued: Exploitations can teach a system about its weaknesses. The faster you discover them, the faster you learn. Tonight's achievement is that Bittensor will invent a locking-based subnet ownership mechanism—specifically, subnet ownership depends on the team's long-term financial investment in the project. This means: 1) Investors can see well in advance whether the owners have unlocked their tokens; 2) They can reprice subnets before the owners; 3) They can flexibly transfer their funds to another team or agency to manage the system. Thanks to @DistStateAndMe for helping to further advance Bittensor's decentralization and developing a solution to one of the oldest problems in the cryptocurrency space: founders deceiving token holders. Looking forward to training some 1T parameter models with expert miners in the field. "The dead never rise again." III. Impact on Bittensor Before its withdrawal, Covenant AI was widely regarded as one of the key growth engines of the Bittensor ecosystem. The Covenant-72B model achieved a major breakthrough: a large language model with 72 billion parameters; collaboratively trained by 70+ independent contributors; completed on general-purpose hardware in a permissionless environment. It was hailed as one of the largest decentralized LLM pre-training experiments in history. Specifically, training a 72 billion parameter model typically requires a tightly coupled cluster of high-end GPUs connected by InfiniBand—hardware that costs tens of millions of dollars and requires latency between nodes to be below milliseconds. Traditionally, it was believed that ordinary internet connections could never bridge this gap. Covenant-72B directly challenges this view, employing SparseLoCo technology—which combines sparsity, 2-bit quantization, and error feedback—resulting in over 146 times less inter-node communication compared to previous distributed operations. The result is that Bittensor's Templar subnet, trained on over 70 peer nodes using 1.1 trillion tokens, and validated using the Gauntlet system, which rewards high-quality nodes (TAO) and penalizes poorly performing nodes using loss assessment and OpenSkill ranking. The endorsement from prominent figures like Jensen Huang and Chamas Palihapitia boosted Bittensor's credibility and strengthened investor confidence in the ecosystem. Therefore, Covenant AI is one of the core narrative drivers in the TAO surge. However, as the event unfolded, the most significant impact on Bittensor was reflected in the market. Following the announcement, the TAO price plummeted and has not yet shown a significant rebound as of this writing. It closed at $264.09, down 17.7%.

Furthermore, as mentioned in Covenant AI, Bittensor suffers from a false sense of decentralization. Therefore, it remains to be seen whether developers and investors will lose confidence in Bittensor. For a long time, Bittensor has been tied to the core narrative of decentralized AI training, and Covenant AI's exit directly impacts the future prospects of this narrative.

Some observers on X pointed out:

  • Will the future of TAO tokens gradually lead to zero due to internal power struggles and divisions within the project team?

    In addition, as mentioned in Covenant AI, Bittensor suffers from a false sense of decentralization.

    Will the future of TAO tokens gradually lead to zero?

    How would you handle the power distribution between subnet owners and the entire ecosystem? I don't care about decentralization: I only care about price.

  • So Sam Dare is the Sam Bankman-Fried of the Bittensor ecosystem.

  • A silly question. Given this governance issue, isn't now the best time to focus on subnets governed by agents rather than humans?

  • The short-term pain of $TAO will eventually bring long-term benefits.

  • We need to fundamentally increase the cost of malicious exits, reduce discontent through legitimate governance, and build a strong enough sense of platform identity so that leaving the platform is a real loss.

Conclusion

In this debate, the concept of decentralization contradicts actual power: Covenant AI expressed its disagreement with centralized power and chose to eventually exit; while Jacob Steeves emphasized mechanism evolution and downplayed the power issue.

In the short term, the market has already reflected this uncertainty in its prices; however, in the long term, the key variable determining TAO's value lies in whether Bittensor can restore governance trust, attract new high-quality subnets, and maintain its decentralized AI narrative.

data.title: Coinbase Partners MarketVector To Launch New Index Combining BTC And Tokenised Gold For Institutional Investors
data.description:
Bitcoin And Tokenised Gold Meet In New Store-Of-Value Benchmark

MarketVector Indexes and Coinbase Asset Management have launched a new benchmark that combines Bitcoin with tokenised gold, aiming to track how investors are thinking about capital preservation in a period of heavy debt and persistent inflation pressure.

The new Coinbase Store of Value Index is built around BTC and Pax Gold (PAXG), one of the largest gold-backed tokens.

It is designed as a price-return index in US dollars and uses a rules-based approach rather than a fixed split between the two assets.

Coinbase and MarketVector launched the Coinbase Store of Value Index, tracking Bitcoin and tokenized gold. It uses a dynamic, rules-based allocation method, adjusting weights based on relative volatility to balance Bitcoin's growth potential with gold's historical stability. pic.twitter.com/YXRkT94bU1

— Bitcoin Bandit (@Birchley23) April 10, 2026

Why The Index Looks Beyond Gold Alone

Gold has long been seen as a safe place for wealth, but Bitcoin is increasingly being treated in a similar way by some investors.

The idea behind the index is to reflect that shift, especially as demand grows for scarce assets that are not tied to government debt or money printing.

Anthony Bassili, President at Coinbase Asset Management, said:

“We believe this index represents a meaningful evolution in store-of-value investing. In a world of fiscal dominance, the future belongs to scarce assets that cannot be printed and are not an obligation of a government or private issuer.”

Gold has long defined “store of value," but that definition is evolving. We’ve partnered with @CoinbaseAM to launch $COINSOV, a rules-based benchmark combining Bitcoin + gold in a volatility-aware framework. Built to help investors navigate today’s macro environment.
Learn more:… pic.twitter.com/AoggNRjwDm

— MarketVector Indexes (@MarketVector) April 9, 2026

How The Allocation Changes

The index does not hold Bitcoin and gold at fixed weights.

Instead, it uses inverse volatility to guide allocation, giving more weight to the asset that has been less volatile at the time.

It is rebalanced every quarter, allowing the mix to adjust as market conditions change.

MarketVector said its research, covering 2017 through 2025, suggests the model delivered better risk-adjusted returns than simpler approaches.

It also showed smaller drawdowns than a basic 50/50 allocation, while outperforming several widely followed benchmarks over the same period.

Why Institutions May Pay Attention

The launch comes as Bitcoin’s store-of-value case faces more scrutiny.

Over the past year, it has often traded more like a risk asset, moving alongside equities at times.

That has prompted some investors to question whether it still behaves like digital gold in all market conditions.

Martin Leinweber, Director of Digital Asset Research and Strategy at MarketVector, said the index reflects rising demand for hybrid investment solutions as macro uncertainty deepens.

Major signal from the indexing world.

MarketVector, the same company that runs the MarketVector Digital Assets 100 Index, just launched the Coinbase Store of Value Index.

It tracks Bitcoin and tokenized gold together.

Not Bitcoin and tech stocks. Not Bitcoin and crypto… pic.twitter.com/DSIGCmSq5P

— Taurus (@Taurus4BTC) April 10, 2026

MarketVector, which is based in Europe and acts as a regulated benchmark administrator, has already expanded beyond traditional indexing into digital assets with products such as the MarketVector Digital Assets 100 Index and the Coinbase 50 Index.

The new benchmark is meant to sit at the crossroads of old and new stores of value, giving institutions a structured way to combine Bitcoin’s upside with gold’s steadier profile.

data.title: Trump’s Mar-a-Lago Memecoin Gala Clashes With Correspondents’ Dinner—Can He Be in Two Places at Once?
data.description:
Will The Mar-a-Lago Gala Be Cancelled Due To Trump's Absence?

A high-profile memecoin gala hosted by Donald Trump at Mar-a-Lago is raising fresh doubts over both scheduling feasibility and ethics, as the event clashes with the White House Correspondents’ Dinner and draws scrutiny from U.S. lawmakers over potential conflicts of interest.

Trump is set to host an exclusive luncheon for top holders of his Official TRUMP memecoin at his Florida estate on April 25—the same day he is expected to attend the White House Correspondents’ Dinner in Washington, D.C.

ABD’de $Trump bağlantılı memecoin için 25 Nisan’da Mar-a-Lago’da özel bir etkinlik düzenlenecek.

Etkinlik, en büyük 297 yatırımcıya açık olacak ve Trump’ın konuşmacı olarak yer alacağı şekilde tanıtılıyor.

Ancak Trump’ın programa katılıp katılmayacağı net değil.

Aynı gün… pic.twitter.com/IpZKpksiWi

— Coinkolik (@Coinkolik) April 9, 2026

The gala is marketed as an all-day experience beginning in the morning, featuring a VIP reception and champagne toast with Trump for the top 29 token holders. Invitations are tied to a leaderboard ranking participants based on their time-weighted holdings, effectively linking token ownership to access.

However, a disclosure published on the event's website that stated "Trump might not be able to attend the conference" has raised eyebrows, with some speculating that the event itself could even be canceled or rescheduled.

In such cases, participants could instead receive a limited-edition TRUMP-themed NFT, raising further questions about the reliability of the promised in-person access.

Despite the apparent scheduling conflict, organizers have maintained that Trump’s attendance at both engagements is expected.

"Of course, the President will be attending both events. After the conference at Mar-A-Largo, the President will fly back to Washington for the White House Correspondents' dinner."

UPDATE: Donald Trump faces a scheduling clash as his $TRUMP memecoin gala at Mar-a-Lago coincides with the White House Correspondents’ Dinner, raising questions over attendance and event plans.

Politics meets crypto again. What’s your take on this crossover?…

— Roundtable Network (@RTB_io) April 9, 2026

Trump had previously confirmed his participation at the correspondents dinner, which would mark his first appearance after boycotting the event throughout his first term. Still, the tight turnaround between Florida and Washington has fueled skepticism, particularly given Trump’s brief appearance at a similar memecoin-linked gala in 2025.

That earlier event, held at a Trump golf property, drew protests and political backlash, with Trump reportedly staying for only about 20 minutes—adding to doubts about how much access token holders may realistically receive.

Lawmakers question whether memecoin is “selling access”

Beyond logistics, the event has triggered a sharper political response. Senators Elizabeth Warren, Adam Schiff, and Richard Blumenthal have raised concerns that the memecoin project may be leveraging Trump’s appearance to drive demand for the token.

🇺🇲 SEN. ELIZABETH WARREN TARGETS TRUMP-BACKED $WLFI, CALLS IT A MAJOR CORRUPTION SCANDAL 🚨

“President Trump’s crypto company is the most disgraceful presidential corruption scandal in history.”

She also warned:
“Anyone who owns 10% or more of World Liberty Financial must be… pic.twitter.com/tUA842kQPV

— COACHTY (@TheRealTRTalks) February 27, 2026

In a letter to promoter Bill Zanker, the lawmakers questioned whether organizers were effectively “dangling access” to the president to encourage purchases that could generate fees benefiting Trump and his associates—particularly given the uncertainty around his attendance.

The scrutiny comes amid broader tensions in Washington over crypto regulation and ethics, with lawmakers increasingly focused on how digital asset ventures intersect with political influence and financial gain.

The controversy has unfolded alongside renewed volatility in the TRUMP token. Following the announcement of the second gala in March, the token surged from roughly $2.70 to as high as $4.40 before settling back near the $3 range.

The structure of the event—where higher token holdings translate into closer proximity to Trump—has intensified criticism that financial participation is being directly tied to political access.

As questions mount, the memecoin gala is emerging as a flashpoint in the evolving relationship between politics and crypto markets. Whether Trump ultimately attends both events or not, the combination of scheduling uncertainty, financial incentives, and ethical concerns is likely to keep the spotlight firmly on the initiative.

data.title: DeepSeek V4: Everything You Need to Know About the New Trillion-Parameter AI Model
data.description:
Source: DeepSeek Official Website, Compiled by: Jinse Finance

The field of artificial intelligence is undergoing a major transformation. DeepSeek V4 is DeepSeek's upcoming flagship model, which not only breaks through the boundaries of parameter scale but also achieves unprecedented high efficiency. This model is expected to have 1 trillion (1T) parameters, natively support multimodal operation, and is equipped with 1 million token context windows, directly competing with products from Western tech giants such as OpenAI's GPT-5.4 and Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.5.

In this comprehensive analysis, we will delve into the core specifications, architectural innovations, expected pricing, and the strategic hardware deployment shift behind DeepSeek V4.

In this comprehensive analysis, we will analyze the core specifications, architectural innovations, expected pricing, and the strategic hardware deployment shift behind DeepSeek V4.

Key Highlights

  • Adopts MoE architecture, trillion-parameter scale, but only activates approximately 32-37 billion parameters when processing each token

  • 1 million token context windows, equivalent to the text volume of 15-20 novels

  • Native multimodal capabilities: supports text, image, video, and audio processing from the underlying layer

  • API pricing is only 1/10 to 1/50 of GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus 4.5

  • Model weights are expected to be based on Apache 2.0

  • Open source license. Local deployment and operation are supported by dual RTX 4090 graphics cards or a single RTX 5090 graphics card. What is DeepSeek V4? Building on the success of its predecessors (such as V3 and R1), DeepSeek V4 combines massive scalability with extreme cost-effectiveness. The following are its main technical parameters:

    Three Breakthrough Architectural Innovations

    The true strength of DeepSeek V4 lies not only in its powerful computing power, but also in three specific architectural breakthroughs that make training and running trillion-parameter models possible and cost-controllable.

    1. Engram Conditional Memory

    The biggest challenge faced by a million-token context window is accurately retrieving information without a sharp increase in computing costs. DeepSeek solves this problem through the Engram architecture.

    This system separates static facts (such as API signatures or specific patterns) from dynamic reasoning. In the "Needle-in-a-Haystack" benchmark (finding specific facts among 1 million tokens), Enggram dramatically improved accuracy from a normal 84.2% to an astonishing 97%. 2. Manifold-Constrained Hyperconnected Structures (mHC) As AI models grow in size, they often experience training instability issues (such as gradient explosion). Manifold-constrained hyperconnected structures (mHC) are a mathematical framework that constrains the amplification of signals, keeping it within 2x (compared to up to 3000x in the unconstrained state). This allows DeepSeek to stably train a trillion-parameter model with only 6.7% of the computational cost. 3. DeepSeek DSA Mechanism and Lightning Indexer To efficiently handle the context of 1 million tokens, V4 uses a sparse attention mechanism (DSA) instead of traditional dense attention. The Lightning Indexer can quickly scan the context and locate relevant fragments, and the model only needs to perform attention computation on these specific tokens. In long context scenarios, this solution can reduce computational cost by approximately 50%. Benchmark Comparison: DeepSeek V4 vs GPT-5.4 and Claude 4.5 DeepSeek V4 focuses on software engineering and deep inference capabilities. According to internal benchmark data, the model exhibits outstanding performance. Note: This data is currently based on leaked internal information and will require independent third-party verification after the model's official release. API Pricing: Cutting-Edge AI with the Best Cost-Effectiveness

    Western models such as GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus offer powerful performance, but are expensive to use. For example, GPT-5.2/5.4 costs approximately $1.75 to $15.00 per million input tokens, while Claude Opus 4.5 costs $5.00 per million.

    API Pricing: Cutting-Edge AI with the Best Cost-Effectiveness

    Western models such as GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus 4.5 cost approximately $1.75 to $15.00 per million input tokens.

    API Pricing: Cutting-Edge AI with the Best Cost-Effectiveness

    Western models such as GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus 4.5 cost approximately $ ...1.75 to $15.00 per million input tokens, while Claude Opus 4.5 costs approximately $5.00 per million input tokens.

    API Pricing: Cutting-Edge AI with the Best Cost-Effectiveness

    Western models such as GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus 4.5 cost approximately $1.75 to $15.00 per million input tokens, The expected API pricing for DeepSeek V4 is highly disruptive: This means that, with comparable performance, DeepSeek V4 will cost approximately 1/10 to 1/50 of its Western competitors. Geopolitics and Hardware: Shifting to the Huawei Ecosystem One of the most far-reaching features of DeepSeek V4 lies in its hardware support system. Due to strict US export controls on NVIDIA's high-end GPUs (such as the B300 and H200), DeepSeek has deeply optimized V4, making its inference process primarily rely on domestically produced Chinese chips. While the initial training phase of the model likely still uses NVIDIA hardware (such as the H800), the model has been highly optimized for Huawei's Ascend 950PR and Cambricon's MLU chip. Reportedly, the computing performance of Huawei's Ascend 950PR is 2.87 times that of NVIDIA's H20 (currently a chip legally allowed to be exported to China). This marks a significant milestone for China in achieving self-sufficiency in AI chips. Release Date: When will DeepSeek V4 be released? DeepSeek V4 has experienced several delays. Initially rumored to launch in mid-February 2026 during the Lunar New Year, the release was delayed due to significant engineering challenges in training on the new hardware architecture. On March 9, 2026, an unreleased "V4 Lite" version (approximately 200 billion parameters) appeared on the DeepSeek platform, effectively validating the feasibility of its core architecture. It is now widely expected that the full trillion-parameter version will be officially released in April 2026. Following release, DeepSeek is expected to release the model weights under the Apache 2.0 open-source license. Thanks to the efficiency of the MoE architecture and quantization techniques (INT8/INT4), this ultra-large-scale model is expected to run natively on consumer-grade hardware, such as dual RTX 4090 graphics cards or a single RTX 5090 graphics card.

    DeepSeek V4 Frequently Asked Questions

    1. Is DeepSeek V4 better than ChatGPT (GPT-5.4)?

    According to internal benchmark results, in complex software engineering tasks (SWE-bench) and codebase-level programming tasks, DeepSeek V4 performs comparably to GPT-5.4 and Claude 4.5, and even slightly outperforms them, especially with its million-token context window. However, after the model is officially released, independent testing is still needed to determine the absolute winner.

    2. Can I run DeepSeek V4 locally?

    Yes, provided that the model weights are open-sourced as planned.

    Because the MoE architecture activates only about 32 billion parameters at a time, the quantized version (INT4) can theoretically run locally on a single 32GB RTX 5090 or dual RTX 4090 graphics cards. 3. Why is DeepSeek V4 so low-cost? Its low cost stems from the efficient MoE architecture (activating only a portion of the model's parameters), low training costs (estimated at around $10 million, while similar Western models cost over $100 million), and the application of the DeepSeek DSA mechanism—a technology that halves the computational power required for long contexts. 4. What does V4's so-called "native multimodal" mean? Unlike earlier models that used a "late-stage fusion" approach by "stitching" visual plugins onto text models, V4 is jointly trained from the outset on text, images, video, and audio. This gives it superior cross-modal reasoning capabilities, such as seamlessly generating corresponding interface code directly from hand-drawn architecture diagrams. Conclusion: DeepSeek V4 is not just an incremental update, but a breakthrough at the architectural level. It proves that intelligent optimization (Engram memory architecture, mHC hyperconnection structure, DSA mechanism) can completely rival high-cost, brute-force parameter scaling methods. With its native multimodal capabilities, million-token context window, and disruptive pricing strategy, DeepSeek is forcing enterprises and independent developers to re-evaluate their AI infrastructure. Once the API and open-source weights are launched as planned in April 2026, the overall landscape of artificial intelligence development may undergo a fundamental transformation.

data.title: Liang Wenfeng revealed internally that DeepSeek V4 will be released in late April.
data.description:
Author: Zhang Chi, Source: Phoenix Technology "Innovation Notes"

After several rumors of delays, the highly anticipated DeepSeek V4 is about to be released.

According to "Innovation Notes" from multiple sources, DeepSeek founder Liang Wenfeng recently revealed in an internal communication that DeepSeek's new flagship large model, DeepSeek V4, will be officially released in late April.

Previously, reports indicated that DeepSeek V4 would not only bring trillions of parameters and millions of context windows, but also achieve deep compatibility with domestically produced chips such as Huawei's Ascend for the first time, marking a crucial step forward for China's AI industry on the path of "de-CUDAization." It's worth noting that on the evening of March 29th, DeepSeek experienced a full 13-hour service outage. At the time, the official explanation was only "server failure," but many developers discovered significant changes in model output logic after the service was restored: the coding structure was clearer, the logical rigor was dramatically improved, and the SVG graphics generation capability was significantly enhanced. Industry insiders generally speculate that this "shutdown" was actually a gray-scale test or full deployment of V4. It is understood that V4 will be open-sourced under the Apache 2.0 license, allowing enterprises to deploy it independently. According to the latest foreign media reports, in preparation for the V4 release, domestic tech giants such as Alibaba, ByteDance, and Tencent have pre-ordered hundreds of thousands of next-generation AI computing chips, planning to provide DeepSeek's new models through cloud services and integrate them into their own AI products. As a result, the price of the new AI chips has recently increased by about 20%. Previously, the release date of DeepSeek had been delayed multiple times. Originally planned for a high-parameter version around mid-February, before the Spring Festival, it was postponed due to reasons such as deep adaptation to domestic computing power, architecture restructuring, and stability refinement. Liang Wenfeng, who transitioned from quantitative investment to AI, is undergoing his own "transformation." According to LatePost, although some core members of the DeepSeek team left in the past year (including R1 core author Guo Daya), "some left, but more stayed," and the team did not suffer a complete loss. Since the second half of 2025, Liang Wenfeng has begun to emphasize productization and commercialization more, building a product team, recruiting "model strategy product managers" specializing in Agents, and extending from pure model development to the application layer. Simultaneously, he is valuing the company to clarify the value of employee stock options and provide the team with more certain expectations. Facing strong competition from rivals such as Doubao, Qianwen, Zhipu, Kimi, and MiniMax, the V4, released several months late, carries immense pressure and expectations—can Liang Wenfeng once again create the world's best open-source large model? It is understood that V4's biggest technological highlight lies in its breakthrough in LTM (Long Term Memory) capabilities. However, several AI entrepreneurs who have had in-depth collaborations with DeepSeek told *Chuangzhi Ji* that the industry should not have too high expectations; V4 is unlikely to replicate the influence of DeepSeek-V3 last year. In addition to DeepSeek V4, the Hunyuan 3.0 large-scale model will also be officially released in April. This is Tencent's first major large-scale model product since the team restructuring in the second half of 2025 and the appointment of Yao Shunyu as Chief AI Scientist.

data.title: Tiger Research: South Korea Cryptocurrency Market Guide 2026
data.description:
Author: Ryan Yoon, Research Analyst; Source: Tiger Research; Translated by: Shaw Jinse Finance

Key Takeaways

  • The South Korean cryptocurrency market is at a structural turning point. While boasting a large base of 11 million registered investors, retail participation is declining as institutional capital begins to fill the gap.

  • There's much more going on beneath the surface than the headlines suggest. Marketing strategies have been public knowledge for some time. Long-time low-profile South Korean developers are increasingly turning to artificial intelligence. Even before the relevant legislation was finalized, institutions were already allocating to Korean won stablecoins.

  • The situation in 2026 is no longer suitable for last year's strategies.

  • Retail investors are exhausted, while institutional investors are still figuring things out. Teams that are the first to build the future landscape will seize the opportunities of the next phase. 1. What Makes South Korean Investors Different? To understand the South Korean cryptocurrency market, the key is to understand its investors. South Korean consumers are among the world's most willing to accept and use new digital services. OpenAI ranked South Korea as the largest ChatGPT market in the Asia-Pacific region in September 2025, while Anthropic's economic index report released in January 2026 ranked South Korea as one of the countries with the highest per capita Claude usage globally. South Korea quickly adopts and uses new technologies. This model appeared earlier in the cryptocurrency field than in the artificial intelligence field. South Korean investors hold a significant share of altcoin trading and are actively involved in the ecosystem of emerging projects. Cryptocurrencies were central to the market long before the advent of artificial intelligence. However, market sentiment has shifted. For years, tired narratives and ultimately unsuccessful projects have bored investors. Simultaneously, the structural environment is undergoing unprecedented changes: stock market policies are tightening, institutional funds are flowing in in large quantities, and regulatory frameworks are gradually taking shape. The market is maturing, and the investor base is weary—these two factors combined make it necessary to re-examine this market. 2. Overview of Cryptocurrency Investors

    In South Korea, cryptocurrency trading is mainly concentrated on exchanges denominated in Korean Won. As of the end of 2025, the number of verified users reached a record high of 11.13 million. However, the growth rate is slowing. After peaking at 24.7% in the second half of 2024, it dropped to 11% in the first half of 2025 and further decreased to 5.2% in the second half. The number of new users is decreasing.

    Average daily trading volume fell to approximately $3.7 billion, a 15% decrease compared to the same period last year, while exchange operating profit declined by 38% year-on-year. Despite an increase in registered participants, actual trading activity shrank. The broader context is a bull market in stocks. The Korea Composite Stock Price Index (KOSPI) opened at approximately 2400 points in January 2025 and broke through 6300 points in February 2026, more than doubling in just over a year.

    During the same period, the gap between cryptocurrency trading volume and stock market trading volume continued to widen. This difference has been particularly significant since the beginning of January this year. This does not mean that investors have lost interest in cryptocurrencies, but rather that investors have more options.

    3. Five Must-Know Industries in South Korea

    3.1. Cryptocurrency Exchanges: Can the Duopoly Be Maintained? The South Korean cryptocurrency exchange market remains firmly dominated by two giants—Upbit and Bithumb. Together, they hold approximately 87% of the market share, with Coinone close behind at around 10%. Recently, the combined impact of regulatory pressure and mergers and acquisitions has begun to shake this格局 (structure/pattern). Three things are worth watching this year. Will the merger between Naver Financial and Dunamu be completed? The May 2026 shareholder meeting will be a decisive moment. If more than 8% of Dunamu shareholders vote against the deal, it will fall through. Price volatility in the approximately $2.1 billion worth of digital assets held by Dunamu is also an uncertainty. What is the real impact of the partial suspension of Bithumb's operations? This is not an exchange shutdown. It only restricts new users from making external withdrawals for six months. Existing users can continue to use the service normally. What does Mirae Asset's acquisition of Korbit mean for the future? The target model is vertical integration: Mirae Asset tokenizes physical assets such as real estate and bonds, while Korbit handles distribution. 3.2. GTM: How to Survive the Crypto Winter Most participants in the South Korean cryptocurrency market are marketing agencies (GTM). Their services are largely similar: KOL marketing, blog management, Naver SEO, viral community marketing, public relations, and YouTube promotion. Due to the low barriers to entry, new entrants are constantly emerging. The foundation chooses marketing agencies for two reasons. One factor is unfamiliarity with local market dynamics, but the core reason is the difficulty of maintaining an internal operations team. While the services may appear similar on the surface, actual execution requires significant effort, and subtle language differences often determine the final outcome. The problem is that as the industry matures, this model is no longer a secret. The "Korean trick" is widely known, and the foundation is well aware of it. Even when marketing agencies develop strategies, the actual operations are often outsourced. Therefore, some foundations simply bypass agencies and sign contracts directly with KOLs. Especially since last year, KOLs have increasingly formed joint agencies. They operate as DAO-like teams, providing end-to-end marketing solutions as a unified entity. Established marketing firms are adjusting their strategies to meet the challenges. Management depth and strategic capabilities remain significant advantages, but not all clients prioritize quality. To stand out, existing firms are exploring product development, expanding research content, and other avenues. Without a clear competitive advantage, it's increasingly difficult to persuade foundations already familiar with established strategies. Three things to watch this year: KOL Groups: Will They Replace Agencies? KOLs are moving beyond channel management and directly promoting strategies in groups. For foundations, this means they can execute independently without relying on agency profits. The coordinating role previously played by agencies is being rapidly replaced. What impact will the extended TGE delay have on agency revenue? A delayed TGE release will reduce basic marketing budgets. Marketing agencies based on short-term contracts will lose stable revenue streams. Without adjusting contract models, they will struggle to maintain operations. Where do advertising companies go when services are highly homogenized? When service content is monotonous, competition boils down to price. Only advertising companies with unique, hard-to-replicate capabilities (such as proprietary products or research content) can maintain their pricing. 3.3. Korean Won Stablecoins: Who Will Lead the Way? A stablecoin market for the Korean won is taking shape. Despite a legislative deadlock in the National Assembly, banks have already begun to act. The core issue is who has the right to issue bonds. The Financial Supervisory Commission (FSC) and the Bank of Korea insist that only bank-controlled consortia (holding more than 50% of the shares) should be allowed to issue bonds. The Democratic Party working group opposes this, arguing that fintech companies and platform companies should also have issuance rights. No final draft of the relevant legislation has yet been completed. Even so, banks are prioritizing partnerships, believing they will maintain a dominant position regardless of the legislative outcome. Shinhan Financial and Hana Financial are in talks with Samsung to form a consortium. Hana Financial is the fastest-moving of the four major financial groups, having already signed memorandums of understanding with BNK Financial, iM Financial, and SC First Bank. If Samsung Wallet and its global distribution network are also included in this structure, the resulting alliance could potentially control both online and offline payment infrastructure. KB Financial is in talks with domestic player Toss and international player Circle, aiming to acquire both card issuance technology and a distribution platform. K-bank has announced its intention to join the card issuance alliance and leverage its BC Card payment network as a competitive advantage. Toss Bank, with 30 million users, is focusing on developing its distribution and ecosystem. The final consortium structure is yet to be determined, but all major financial institutions have entered the competition. Two factors will determine the outcome. First, the legislative outcome. If a bank-centric structure is enshrined in law, the existing competitive landscape of consortia will remain unchanged. If fintech companies are allowed to enter the market, the competitive landscape will be completely transformed. Second, the Bank of Korea. Incoming Governor Shin Hyun-song has indicated that he will prioritize the development of a central bank digital currency (CBDC) and emphasized the Bank of Korea's role in the stablecoin approval process. The faster the second phase of the Han Gang Plan progresses, the smaller the space for private stablecoins will be. Two things are worth noting in the Korean won stablecoin market this year: Can banks lock in issuance rights? The Financial Services Commission (FSC) and the Bank of Korea (BOK) insist on a syndicated loan structure with banks holding 50%+1 shares; the Democratic Party working group is pushing for the inclusion of fintech in the issuance scope. The legislative outcome will either solidify the existing syndicated loan structure or completely reshape the market landscape. Is the Bank of Korea friendly or hostile towards private stablecoins? Incoming Governor Shin Hyun-song has indicated that he will prioritize the development of central bank digital currencies (CBDCs). The faster the second phase of the Han Gang Plan progresses, the more restrictions the private stablecoin market will face. 3.4. Developers: Numerous but Unsung, Now Fewer South Korea boasts a large number of quietly dedicated entrepreneurs. However, since the failure of the Terra-Luna project, it has become increasingly difficult to find South Korean entrepreneurs making their mark on the international stage. They haven't disappeared; rather, they've chosen to remain behind the scenes. The root cause is deliberate concealment. Teams targeting global markets often downplay their South Korean identity. While the trust crisis caused by the Terra-Luna incident is a factor, the more direct reason stems from within South Korea itself: South Korean investors' skepticism towards South Korean projects is deeply ingrained. Paradoxically, one of the largest potential markets that South Korean projects want to penetrate is also one of the most difficult. Reducing South Korean characteristics has become a survival strategy. The founders chose to register companies in Singapore or Dubai, assemble multinational teams, and build projects in a way that obscured their Korean origins. A deeper problem lies in the shrinking, already obscure community of cryptocurrency developers. Developers with Web3 experience are moving towards AI agents and on-chain AI infrastructure. This shift is natural, but its side effect is a sharp decline in the number of pure cryptocurrency developers. Those developers who were once numerous but little-known have indeed dwindled in number.

    3.5 Academic Groups: Talent Pool and Industry Networks

    SNU Decipher and KAIST Orakle are prime examples. They are not ordinary study groups. Both organizations regularly host research presentations, product showcases, and hackathons, with numerous industry giants directly sponsoring them.

    What makes them unique is their composition. Solely student-run societies are the exception, not the norm. Besides university students, developers, product managers, and financial professionals interested in cryptocurrency also participate.

    These organizations simultaneously serve as talent development channels and industry exchange centers. This is why global chains actively support Korean academic groups: they provide a unified channel for recruiting core developers and cultivating on-chain builders. Many countries have blockchain academic institutions, but in South Korea, the model of active participation by professionals and students is particularly prominent. This model appears to have formed spontaneously in the absence of formal channels to absorb the public's high level of interest in cryptocurrencies. 4. The South Korean Cryptocurrency Market: It's Time to Re-examine It Retail investor interest in the South Korean market is cooling, and data confirms this. Trading volumes are declining, and new user growth is slowing. However, institutional investor activity, including in Korean won stablecoins, is more active than ever, and regulatory discussions are becoming increasingly concrete. Retail investors are shrinking, while institutional investors are stepping in. This transformation appears healthy. Retail investors historically exit the market during cryptocurrency winters. What's truly worrying is the market being empty, but institutional investors are currently rapidly filling those gaps. The new developments resulting from this turning point will shape the next phase of the South Korean cryptocurrency market. For foundations, the clearest opportunity right now lies in targeting institutional clients. Even so, the retail market cannot be completely ignored. Users who have experienced market cycles are no longer interested in familiar narratives. The same strategies will no longer work. To seize the opportunities in this market, one must first understand its complete structure. The South Korean market remains vast and its growth rate remains rapid. However, traditional investment strategies are no longer effective. Retail investors are weak, and institutional investors are still figuring things out. Opportunities lie between the two. Teams that are the first to recognize these opportunities will lead the next stage of development.

data.title: Wall Street Holds Emergency Meeting: Claude's New Model Poses Huge Risks to the Financial Industry
data.description:
Author: Long Yue, Wall Street Insights

Anthropic's latest AI model, Mythos, has triggered high alert from US regulators—two key figures in US financial regulation have joined forces to urgently summon CEOs of top Wall Street banks to Washington.

According to the latest report from Bloomberg, US Treasury Secretary Bessant and Federal Reserve Chairman Powell urgently convened a meeting with CEOs of major Wall Street banks at the Treasury Department headquarters in Washington on Tuesday.The core topic of the meeting was the potential cybersecurity threats posed by Anthropic's latest AI model, Mythos.

The fact that this meeting was arranged on short notice and not publicly reported beforehand is itself a signal: regulators consider new types of cyberattacks to be one of the biggest risks currently facing the financial industry. All invited banks are classified as systemically important financial institutions by top regulatory agencies, and their stability is directly related to the global financial system. Who came, who didn't come? According to sources who spoke to the media, the bank CEOs attending the meeting included: Jane Fraser of Citigroup, Ted Pick of Morgan Stanley, Brian Moynihan of Bank of America, Charlie Scharf of Wells Fargo, and David Solomon of Goldman Sachs. JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon was absent. Spokespeople for all banks declined to comment, and an Anthropic representative did not immediately respond. This lineup almost encompasses the largest systemically important banks in the United States. The regulators' choice to speak directly with the CEO level, rather than at the technical or compliance level, demonstrates the urgency of this warning. Just how dangerous is Mythos? According to Anthropic, Mythos can, under user instructions, "identify and exploit vulnerabilities in every major operating system and web browser." This means that if this model falls into the hands of malicious actors, its attack surface will cover almost all digital infrastructure. For banks heavily reliant on digital systems, this is not an abstract risk—once a core system is compromised, transaction disruptions, data breaches, and even systemic chain reactions are all possible. From real-world data, Mythos's capabilities are not exaggerated. Previously reported, the model discovered a 27-year-old remote crash vulnerability in OpenBSD and a 16-year-old vulnerability in FFmpeg that had been scanned over 5 million times by automated tools but never triggered an alert. Furthermore, it autonomously chained multiple vulnerabilities in the Linux kernel, constructing a complete attack chain from escalating ordinary user privileges to full machine control. For this reason, Anthropic has adopted an extremely cautious approach to its release strategy, currently only opening it to a select few leading technology and financial institutions, including Amazon, Apple, and JPMorgan Chase. These institutions are jointly participating in "Project Glasswing," which aims to be the first to harden the security of critical systems before similar AI models become more widely available. Anthropic stated that it had communicated with U.S. officials regarding Mythos's "offensive and defensive cyber capabilities" prior to the recent release. "Too Powerful" to Be Publicly Discreet: "Project Glasswing" Defenders Take the Lead. As previously mentioned, on Tuesday, April 7th, Anthropic announced the establishment of an industry consortium called "Project Glasswing," collaborating with companies such as Amazon, Apple, Microsoft, and Cisco to provide tools and utilize its next-generation cutting-edge model, Claude Mythos Preview, for vulnerability scanning and remediation of critical software infrastructure. Anthropic stated that due to the immense power of this model, there are currently no plans to release it to the public. Against this backdrop, Project Glasswing is positioned as a preemptive defensive operation, prioritizing its use for defensive purposes before equivalent capabilities are disseminated to a wider range of actors. Anthropic stated that it had been in ongoing communication with US government officials regarding its "cybersecurity capabilities" prior to the release of Mythos. JPMorgan Chase Chief Information Security Officer Pat Opet stated that the bank will evaluate the tool's value in defending critical financial infrastructure in a "rigorous and independent manner." It is worth noting that Anthropic is currently embroiled in a legal dispute with the Trump administration. The Pentagon designated Anthropic as a supply chain risk, a designation Anthropic disputed. This week, a federal appeals court rejected Anthropic's request to suspend the designation. This context makes the relationship between regulators and Anthropic more delicate—on the one hand, the Treasury and the Federal Reserve view its model as a systemic risk requiring urgent attention; on the other hand, Anthropic still faces legal pressure at the government level.

data.title: Binance Launches Zero-Fee Prediction Markets, Letting Users Trade Outcomes for Free
data.description:
Binance Bets On “Free” Trading To Capture The $20 Billion Market Share

Binance is making a high-impact entry into the prediction markets sector, integrating event-based trading into its Wallet app while eliminating trading and settlement fees. The move positions the exchange to compete directly with incumbents like Polymarket and Kalshi in a rapidly expanding market now handling billions in monthly volume.

Binance just killed every barrier to prediction markets in one move.

Gas fees, bridging, wallet setup, desktop-only access - gone.@BinanceWallet × @predictdotfun, a full prediction market now lives inside the Binance App.

→ Gas fees? Binance covers 100%. You pay nothing. Not… https://t.co/QxvXyXy4Ujpic.twitter.com/Cp1smHGNnT

— shmidt (@shmidtqq) April 9, 2026

At the core of Binance’s push is a simple but aggressive strategy: remove friction entirely. Through its integration with Predict.fun, the platform allows users to trade on outcome-based markets without paying gas, trading, or settlement fees, effectively creating a zero-cost entry point for retail participants.

Built on the BNB Smart Chain, the system enables users to speculate on real-world events ranging from politics to sports, while Binance absorbs the underlying transaction costs. The “gasless” experience removes one of the biggest barriers in onchain trading, particularly for newer users unfamiliar with blockchain mechanics.

Stake your take across crypto, politics, sports, and more.

Prediction Markets are now live, thanks to @predictdotfun’s seamless integration with Binance Wallet.

Learn more 👉 https://t.co/NhmLXYLqR8pic.twitter.com/bBG5cUcmLD

— Binance (@binance) April 9, 2026

This model mirrors tactics previously used by exchanges to bootstrap liquidity, but its application in prediction markets marks a notable escalation. By subsidizing all transaction costs, Binance is not only lowering the barrier to entry but also directly undercutting competitors that rely on fee-based models.

Binance’s timing coincides with a surge in prediction market activity, with platforms like Polymarket seeing volumes climb to an estimated $20 billion in a single month during peak periods. The growth has been fueled by rising interest in event-based trading tied to elections, geopolitics, and macroeconomic developments.

Prediction markets are on fire 🔥

Polymarket just surpassed Kalshi in weekly volume and is reportedly valued around $20B.

Largest platforms by weekly volume:
1.Polymarket — $1.93B
2.Kalshi — $1.87B
3.Probable — $133M
4.Opinion — $132M
5.Predict Fun — $55M pic.twitter.com/YdV945qLws

— Nabil Abdullah (@NabilMinhaz) April 5, 2026

By embedding prediction markets directly into its Wallet app, Binance is effectively turning its massive user base into a distribution channel for event contracts. This gives it a structural advantage over standalone platforms, which must acquire users independently while navigating regulatory uncertainty.

The zero-fee model further intensifies competition. While existing platforms have focused on liquidity and market depth, Binance is targeting accessibility and scale, aiming to onboard a broader retail audience through a frictionless trading experience.

Regulatory tensions loom as adoption accelerates

The expansion comes as prediction markets face increasing scrutiny, particularly in the United States. Regulators have raised concerns over whether event-based contracts resemble unlicensed gambling products, with ongoing disputes involving federal and state authorities.

The Commodity Futures Trading Commission has asserted jurisdiction over the sector, while platforms like Kalshi have drawn political attention through high-profile affiliations and regulatory challenges. These tensions underscore the uncertain legal environment surrounding the industry’s rapid growth.

Nevada judge extends Kalshi ban, calls sports contracts 'indistinguishable' from gambling. Nevada is now the only state with a court-enforced, in-effect ban against the New York-based company.

Via @TheBlockCohttps://t.co/hfLZkUB79w

— Daniel Wallach (@WALLACHLEGAL) April 5, 2026

Against this backdrop, Binance’s global footprint may provide a strategic advantage, allowing it to scale outside of the most restrictive jurisdictions while the regulatory landscape continues to evolve.

Binance’s entry signals a broader shift as major exchanges move to capture a share of the fast-growing prediction market economy. Once considered a niche segment, event-based trading is increasingly being viewed as a core primitive for information markets and price discovery.

By combining zero-fee access, seamless integration, and its existing infrastructure, Binance is positioning itself not just as a participant but as a potential gatekeeper for mainstream adoption. The strategy suggests a future where prediction markets are no longer standalone platforms, but embedded features within larger financial ecosystems.

As competition intensifies, the race may ultimately hinge on who can scale fastest. With its decision to eliminate fees entirely, Binance is making a clear bet: that in the next phase of the market, distribution and accessibility—not pricing—will determine the winner.
